chunk_map.heading: # Test and learn (title slide, no lede)
Gap analysis: walk your system against the task
Cancel is legitimate; traces are data (incl. closing kicker)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 2 line 12 "Run two goes packaged, after you've read the return" reads as ordinary descriptive language (parallel to chunk 1's "the result is data") given the apparatus/setup/assay/data metaphor established one slide earlier — resolvable without needing the sibling lecture reading-the-return.md. chunk 4's closer (line 27, "Walk your system. Fill the gaps. Send it off. Read what comes back.") reuses "your system"/"the gaps" from chunk 3, the immediately preceding slide, and "it"/"what comes back" from chunk 1's send-off framing — sequential reuse across slides of the same file, the explicitly NOT-a-violation case.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No "the X you did/saw/caught" backward beat references appear in the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No guess-before-reveal / predict instruction appears anywhere in the body.

chunk 4's closing kicker (line 27) is a whole-lecture recap immediately following the cancel/traces content, functioning as the deck's closer beat rather than breaking the header's summary of the slide's main content.

Checked all five sub-items across all chunks: chunk 3's "Not a template. A question." is a two-part antithesis, not the banned three-part "Not X. Not Y. Z." construction; no anaphora/epistrophe triples; no self-grading superlatives; no number-plus-retraction ("fifteen to thirty minutes" is a stated range, not a hedge-then-retract); no abstraction-noun stacks (apparatus/setup/assay/data in chunk 1 is a defining metaphor list); no empty-leg verb triplets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: "send-off," "subagent," "gap analysis," and "trace" are established student-facing AE101 vocabulary (used throughout run-the-first-experiment.md and walk-and-send-off.md), not maintainer/repo-only jargon like surface, firing, compendium, forcing function, beat.
